# Spin up the next cohort {#sec-new-iteration}

::: {.callout-tip}
**Repeat instructor** — this chapter is the short version. It assumes you already understand the three-org model (@sec-three-orgs) and have a working `gitforsci-dev` with templates. If this is your first time, read Parts 2 and 3 in full instead.
:::

You have taught the course before. The templates in `gitforsci-dev` hold your latest material. Here is the condensed path to the next cohort.

## What carries forward and what stays frozen

Before you start, hold the two rules in mind:

- **Templates carry forward.** The new cohort is generated fresh from `gitforsci-dev`, so it inherits every improvement you have made since the last cohort.
- **Past cohorts stay frozen.** Existing iteration orgs are independent repositories, not forks. Editing the template does not touch them. That is intentional: each past cohort is the archival record of how the course was taught that time.

If you need a template fix inside a cohort that already exists, apply it there as an ordinary commit; it will not flow in automatically.

## The run-book

Replace the iteration tag `002` with the new cohort's tag throughout.

### 1. Update the templates first

- [ ] Make any teaching improvements in `gitforsci-dev` (the website template and the material repositories) before generating the new cohort, so the cohort picks them up.

### 2. Create the iteration org

- [ ] Create the `gitforsci-002` organization on GitHub (organizations are created in the browser).
- [ ] Confirm you are an owner.

### 3. Generate the cohort website

- [ ] On `gitforsci-dev/website`, click **Use this template**, then create `gitforsci-002/website`.
- [ ] Clone it in RStudio and fill in `_variables.yml` and `_quarto.yml` with this cohort's dates, URLs, and labels (@sec-website).
- [ ] Render with `quarto preview`, then publish.

### 4. Create the per-cohort material repositories

- [ ] Use **Use this template** to copy each exercise and assignment repository from `gitforsci-dev` into `gitforsci-002`.

### 5. Sync the citation

- [ ] Copy the canonical `CITATION.cff` from `gitforsci/website` into the new cohort's repositories so they all cite the course the same way (@sec-citation-license).
- [ ] After the cohort runs, create a release of `gitforsci-002/website` so this iteration gets its own DOI alongside the canonical one (@sec-citation-license).

### 6. Invite and verify

- [ ] Invite learners (from the new cohort's survey) to `gitforsci-002` with the `ghclass` scripts in `gitforsci-dev/admin` (@sec-create-orgs).
- [ ] Switch to your test-learner account and verify the member view, a clone, and a commit (@sec-create-orgs).

### 7. Run the pre-flight

- [ ] Work through @sec-before for this cohort: pre-work, survey, dry-run, morning checks.

That is the whole loop. The work is mostly generating fresh copies and filling in this cohort's specifics; the teaching material itself comes along for free from the templates.
